subject: Physics
title: Dark Energy: The Accelerating Cosmos
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Dark Energy: The Accelerating Cosmos.

hook.text: In 1998, astronomers discovered that the universe's expansion is speeding up—not slowing down as everyone expected.

buildup.text: Two teams measured distant supernovae to track how the expansion rate has changed over time.

discovery.text: The supernovae were dimmer than expected—meaning they were farther away. The expansion is accelerating.

twist.text: Something is pushing space apart faster and faster. We call it dark energy but have no idea what it is.

climax.text: Dark energy makes up 68% of the universe. It won Perlmutter, Schmidt, and Riess the 2011 Nobel Prize.

punchline.text: The universe is speeding up. Something invisible is pushing. We don't know what.

quiz.question: What did the 1998 supernova observations reveal?
options: The expansion of the universe is accelerating
Dark matter is slowing the expansion
